title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware for Nested Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: You are given a Node.js Express application that serves static files and provides dynamic routes. However, the route parameter validation is broken for nested routes. Your task is to fix the bug in the middleware function that handles route parameter validation.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be in the format of a URL path, e.g., '/users/:username/posts/:postid'
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a boolean indicating whether the route parameter validation is correct or not
statement.notes: The middleware function should handle both single-level and nested route parameters

constraints.description: Maximum length of a route parameter

examples.explanation: Correct route parameter validation for a nested route
Incorrect route parameter validation for a single-level route
Correct route parameter validation for a deeply nested route

hints.text: First hint text: Check the middleware function for route parameter validation
Second hint text: Consider using a regular expression to validate the route parameters

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a regular expression to validate the route parameters
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate over all route parameters and check their validity
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution summary: Use a regular expression to validate the route parameters in a single pass
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to validate the route parameters in a single pass, with a time complexity of O(n)
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The regular expression validates all route parameters in a single pass
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling nested route parameters
Mistake 2: Using a brute force approach
